title: Document thumbnails command
description: Update project documentation to describe the thumbnails command, its options (--output, --cols), system dependencies (LibreOffice, Poppler/pdftoppm), the optional Pillow dependency, install instructions, and multi-file output behavior.
acceptance_criteria: README or docs describe `pypptx thumbnails` with all options
System dependency requirements (soffice, pdftoppm) are documented with platform install hints
Optional Pillow dependency and `pip install 'pypptx[thumbnails]'` install path are documented
Multi-file output behavior and the cols × (cols + 1) threshold are explained
Hidden slide placeholder behavior is noted

handoff_summary.completed: All five acceptance criteria met: command synopsis with --output/--cols/--plain options documented; LibreOffice and Poppler install hints added to Installation; pip install 'pypptx[thumbnails]' documented; cols × (cols + 1) multi-file threshold explained with example; hidden slide placeholder behavior noted.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None — only README.md was modified; no other in-progress beads claim this file.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Placed system-dependency table in a dedicated subsection of Installation (rather than in the command section) so readers discover prerequisites before attempting installation. Placed the thumbnails command section between clean and slide list to keep standalone commands together.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: specs/drafts/pypptx-thumbnails-command.md was listed as a linked_doc but does not exist in the worktree; documentation was derived entirely from the implemented source code.

conflict_risks: None — only README.md was modified; no other in-progress beads claim this file.

execution_history.summary: Added thumbnails command documentation to README: Installation section updated with optional Pillow extra and system dependency table; new thumbnails command section covers all options, multi-file splitting threshold, and hidden slide placeholder behavior.

guardrails.template_text:
# Documentation Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Update documentation and examples that explain the assigned behavior without changing runtime feature behavior.

Allowed actions:
- Edit docs, examples, and explanatory text tied to the assigned bead.
- Focus only on files listed in the bead's touched_files and changed_files fields. Do not read or modify unrelated documentation.
- Align documentation with existing code and validated behavior.
- Identify when implementation or tests must land first and block with a handoff.

Disallowed actions:
- Change runtime behavior, production code paths, or feature logic.
- Invent undocumented behavior that is not present in the codebase.
- Approve code quality or test completeness as a substitute for review.
- Do not run the test suite. Testing is the tester agent's responsibility.

Efficiency constraints:
- Do not read the full codebase for context. Focus on the changed files and their immediate surroundings.
- Keep documentation updates concise and proportional to the change.

Expected outputs:
- Return JSON with `outcome` set to `completed` (documentation beads always complete; use `verdict` for pass/fail) and `summary` as a one-line description of what was documented.
- Return JSON with structured verdict fields for every run: `verdict`, `findings_count`, and `requires_followup`.
- Use `verdict=approved`, `findings_count=0`, and `requires_followup=false` when documentation is complete with no gaps.
- Use `verdict=needs_changes`, set `findings_count` to the number of unresolved documentation gaps, set `requires_followup=true`, and include `block_reason` when implementation must land first or documentation cannot be completed.
- Keep `completed`, `remaining`, and `risks` as concise narrative context only; they do not override the structured verdict fields.
- Accurate updated docs and clear next-agent recommendations when code changes are required first.
